translations.en: And with the preceding chain of narration, Ali ibn Mahzyar reported from Ahmad ibn Muhammad, from Hammad ibn Uthman, who said: Ismail al-Jufi said: I heard Abu Jafar Muhammad ibn Ali, peace be upon them both, say:
translations.ur: And with the preceding chain of narration, Ali ibn Mahzyar reported from Ahmad ibn Muhammad, from Hammad ibn Uthman, who said: Ismail al-Jufi said: I heard Abu Jafar Muhammad ibn Ali, peace be upon them both, say:

translations.en: Whoever establishes a just tradition which is then followed, he shall have the like of the reward of all who act upon it, without anything being diminished from their rewards. And whoever establishes an unjust tradition which is then followed, he shall bear the burden of all who act upon it, without anything being diminished from their burdens.

And whoever establishes an unjust tradition which is then followed, he shall bear the burden of all who act upon it, without anything being diminished from their burdens.

narrators.name_en: Abu Jafar Muhammad ibn Ali (Imam al-Baqir)

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: وَبِالْإِسْنَادِ الْأَوَّلِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مَهْزِيَارَ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ قَالَ قَالَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ الْجُعْفِيُّ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِمَا يَقُولُ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: مَنْ سَنَّ سُنَّةَ عَدْلٍ فَاتُّبِعَ كَانَ لَهُ مِثْلُ أَجْرِ مَنْ عَمِلَ بِهَا مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يَنْقُصَ مِنْ أُجُورِهِمْ شَيْءٌ وَمَنْ سَنَّ سُنَّةَ جَوْرٍ فَاتُّبِعَ كَانَ عَلَيْهِ وِزْرُ مَنْ عَمِلَ بِهَا مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يَنْقُصَ مِنْ أَوْزَارِهِمْ شَيْءٌ

en.summary: Imam al-Baqir, peace be upon him, teaches that the moral consequences of introducing customs extend beyond the individual originator. In the social context of early Islam, where community norms shaped collective behavior, this hadith addresses community leaders and influencers, warning that whoever introduces a just practice receives ongoing reward equal to every followers merit, while whoever introduces an unjust practice bears ongoing sin equal to every followers wrongdoing, without any reduction in the followers own accounts.

key_terms.سُنَّة: An established practice or tradition that becomes a norm for others to follow
key_terms.عَدْل: Justice, fairness, and balance in conduct and judgment
key_terms.جَوْر: Injustice, tyranny, and deviation from the right path
key_terms.أَجْر: Divine reward earned through righteous deeds
key_terms.وِزْر: A burden of sin or moral liability for wrongdoing

en.seo_question: What does Islam say about the reward for establishing good practices and the punishment for introducing unjust traditions? How does Imam al-Baqir explain ongoing reward and sin for community leaders?
